response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior individual contributor with 15+ years of software development experience, genuine developer-tooling interests (test runners, assertion libraries, CI/CD setup, MCP code generators), and some mobile exposure (iOS/Apple TV). However, the role is a people-management position requiring at least 2 years of formal team leadership and 3 years of technical leadership, and the resume shows no evidence of managing engineers, conducting performance reviews, or owning team roadmaps. The developer-tools experience is real but scattered across side projects rather than sustained professional focus on build systems or testing infrastructure at scale.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Authentic developer-tooling track record: test runner (Trial), assertion library (Fluent-Asserts), CI/GitLab infrastructure, MCP code generators
Strong engineering-culture contributions: TDD advocacy, workshops, code katas, and technical teaching
concerns: No formal people-management experience — the core requirement of this manager role
No demonstrated experience setting team priorities, roadmaps, or leading organizational change
Mobile-platform experience is limited to app development, not mobile build/test infrastructure at ecosystem scale

key_factors.factor: Deep hands-on engineering and developer-tools background
No strategic roadmap or org-leadership evidence
